name: Bytebot
tagline: Self-hosted AI desktop agent with its own virtual computer
metaDescription: Bytebot is a self-hosted AI desktop agent with its own virtual computer. Give it tasks in plain English and watch it use real apps, files, and browsers.
overview: Bytebot is an open-source AI agent that comes with its own full virtual desktop. Unlike browser-only agents, it can open any application, manage its own file system, log into websites with password managers, and read documents like PDFs and spreadsheets.

You describe a task in plain language and Bytebot completes it the way a person would: it sees the screen, moves the mouse, types on the keyboard, and works across multiple programs. It is self-hosted, so everything runs on your own infrastructure with your own AI provider keys.
The system has four parts that work together: a virtual Ubuntu desktop, an AI agent that controls it, a web task interface where you watch it work, and REST APIs for programmatic control.
features: A complete Ubuntu Linux desktop with pre-installed apps, so the agent can use real browsers, editors, and office tools, not just web pages
Natural-language tasks: describe what you need and Bytebot plans and carries out the multi-step workflow
File uploads so the agent can read and extract data from PDFs, contracts, and spreadsheets you drop onto a task
Live desktop view plus a takeover mode to step in and help or configure something mid-task
Password manager support (1Password, Bitwarden, and others) for automatic login and 2FA handling
Works with Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, and Google Gemini, plus 100+ more providers through LiteLLM

gettingStarted.intro: The fastest way to run Bytebot locally is Docker Compose. Clone the repo, add one AI provider API key, and start the stack.

steps.heading: Clone the repository
steps.body: Get the source and move into the project directory.

steps.heading: Add your AI provider key
steps.body: Choose one provider and write its key into the Docker env file.
steps.code:
echo "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-..." > docker/.env
# Or: echo "OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-..." > docker/.env
# Or: echo "GEMINI_API_KEY=..." > docker/.env

steps.heading: Start Bytebot and open the UI
steps.body: Bring up the stack with Docker Compose, then open the task interface in your browser.

steps.heading: Create a task via the API (optional)
steps.body: You can also create tasks programmatically by posting a description to the REST endpoint.

useCases: Business process automation: invoice processing, data extraction, cross-system data sync, and report generation from multiple sources
Development and testing: automated UI testing, cross-browser checks, and documentation with screenshots
Research and analysis: competitive analysis across websites, document summarization, and market research compilation
Document workflows: upload PDFs or contracts and have the agent read them, extract key terms, and produce a summary report
